privileged_info_summary: The FinancialCalcApp is a synthetic Android project containing three Kotlin classes: LoanCalculator, InvestmentAnalyzer, and BudgetPlanner. The project uses JUnit 4.13.2 and Mockito 5.8.0 as test dependencies. Currently has zero test coverage. LoanCalculator contains 5 methods implementing standard amortization formulas and loan eligibility calculations, with methods returning Double or Boolean. InvestmentAnalyzer contains 5 methods for ROI (formula: (finalValue - initialValue) / initialValue * 100), CAGR, compound interest (A = P(1+r/n)^(nt)), Sharpe ratio (throws ArithmeticException if stdDeviation = 0), and volatility (sample standard deviation). BudgetPlanner contains 5 methods for savings rate (savings / income * 100), emergency fund target (monthlyExpenses * 6 months default), budget allocations, months to goal, and budget variance evaluation. All classes use Kotlin require() to throw IllegalArgumentException for invalid inputs. The task requires writing at least 15 unit test methods covering all three classes with 80% logic coverage and achieving a pass threshold score of 70/100.
